gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is an empty scaffold with all six articles undefined (version 1.0.0-seed, status 'scaffold — awaiting human authorship'). This directly prevents the Mission Statement Protocol from fulfilling its purpose of translating a human-authored BoR into actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules. As a result, the DIO is operating without a constitutional definition of scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, or amendment protocol. The Arbiter cannot enforce a scope perimeter, and Nomos has no MSP rules to evaluate beyond the empty 'default-permissive' bootstrap, leaving governance effectively suspended while the organism continues to process events and transitions autonomously.

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Undefined Organ Availability SLA",
      "description": "The MSP contains no rule specifying required organ uptime percentage or maximum downtime duration. Frequent transitions between ALIVE and DEGRADED are observed (e.g., all organs flipping within seconds), but without an SLA threshold, there is no operational target to enforce resilience or alert on degraded service.",
      "affected_sections": ["MSP Rules (empty)"],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Without SLA definitions, organs may remain DEGRADED indefinitely, compromising DIO integrity and user-facing functionality. No automated escalation or human notification criteria exist.",
      "grounds": "MSP §Rules currently contains no substantive rules. BoR Article II (Prohibited Activities) and Article I (Scope) remain undefined, so no constitutional backstop applies."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Missing Runtime Health Check Cadence Baseline",
      "description": "The spine transitions show rapid oscillations (multiple recoveries and failures within the same minute). There is no defined monitoring interval, retry policy, or minimum pong success rate rule in the MSP. The system appears to react with default-permissive evaluation, but no baseline ensures consistent operational behavior.",
      "affected_sections": ["MSP Rules (empty)", "Genome References (none)"],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Without a cadence baseline, Spine may ping too aggressively or not fast enough, leading to false DEGRADED states or delayed detection. Could cause unnecessary state churn and load on organs.",
      "grounds": "MSP §Genome References is empty; no organ-specific operating rules exist. BoR Article III (Core Identity) and Article VI (Interpretation Rules) offer no guidance on operational cadence."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "No Recovery Time Objective (RTO) Defined",
      "description": "Observed recovery times vary across transitions (e.g., from 0.1s to 30s). Without an RTO rule in the MSP, there is no target for restoring DEGRADED organs to ALIVE, and no enforcement action if recovery is slow.",
      "affected_sections": ["MSP Rules (empty)"],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "Long recovery times could lead to cascading failures or unresponsiveness. No automatic rollback or safe-mode triggers exist to protect system stability.",
      "grounds": "MSP §Rules is empty; BoR Article IV (Human Authority Reservation) is undefined, leaving vague who decides acceptable recovery behaviors."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "No Operational Rules Enforced by Nomos or Arbiter",
      "description": "The MSP seed states 'The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules...' Nomos and Arbiter therefore cannot reject any AP based on operational non-compliance. This permissive state is a gap for every operational dimension.",
      "affected_sections": ["MSP §Rules", "MSP §Relationship to Bill of Rights"],
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "critical",
      "impact": "The DIO has no automated safety net for operational behavior. Any action, including those causing prolonged downtime or security risks, is permitted until human-authored rules are published.",
      "grounds": "MSP explicitly declares no rules; BoR is a scaffold with all articles 'To be defined'. The entire operational governance layer is missing."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-005",
      "title": "Lack of Degraded State Handling Rules",
      "description": "Transitions show roughly simultaneous mass DEGRADED events followed by mass recoveries (e.g., 26 organs flipping at nearly identical timestamps). There is no rule for coordinated recovery sequencing, circuit-breaker thresholds, or degraded-mode escalation paths.",
      "affected_sections": ["MSP Rules (empty)", "Genome References (none)"],
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Mass simultaneous failures may indicate a systemic issue (network partition, resource exhaustion). Without a rule, the system may repeatedly flap without intervention, potentially causing real service disruption.",
      "grounds": "MSP §Genome References is empty; no risk-tier-specific constraints exist. BoR Article V (Amendment Protocol) offers no interim operational safeguard."
    }
  ]
}
